title: The Goa, Daman and Diu Mining Concessions (Abolition and Declaration as Mining Leases) Act, 1987
full_text: The Goa, Daman and Diu Mining Concessions (Abolition and Declaration as Mining Leases) Act, 1987 गोवा, दमण और दीव खनन रियायत (उत्‍सादन और खनन पट्टा के रूप में घोषणा) अधिनियम,1987 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 198716 Act Number: 16 Enactment Date: 1987-05-23 Act Year: 1987 Short Title: The Goa, Daman and Diu Mining Concessions (Abolition and Declaration as Mining Leases) Act, 1987 Hindi Title: गोवा, दमण और दीव खनन रियायत (उत्‍सादन और खनन पट्टा के रूप में घोषणा) अधिनियम,1987 Long Title: An Act to provide for the abolition of the mining concessions in operation in the Union territory of Goa, Daman and Diu and specified in the First and the Second Schedules, and for the declaration of such mining concessions as mining leases under the Mines and Minerals (Regulation and Development) Act, 1957 with a view to the regulation of the mines to which such concessions relate and for the development of minerals under the control of the Union and for matters connected therewith or incidental thereto. Ministry: Ministry of Mines Enforcement Date: 23-05-1987 Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Goa, Daman and Diu Mining Concessions (Abolition and Declaration as Mining Leases) Act, 1987 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPTER II ABOLITION OF MINING CONCESSIONS AND DECLARATION AS MINING LEASES UNDER THE MINES AND MINERALS ACT CHAPTER III PAYMENT OF AMOUNTS CHAPTER IV COMMISSIONER OF PAYMENTS CHAPTER V MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Act to override all other enactments, etc. Section 4. Abolition, etc., of mining concessions. Section 5. General effect of declaring the mining concessions to be mining leases. Section 6. Payment of amounts to concession holders. Section 7. Payment of further amount. Section 8. Commissioner of Payments to be appointed. Section 9. Payment by the Central Government to the Commissioner. Section 10. Claims to be made to the Commissioner. Section 11. Liability of Central Government to end on payment of claim. Section 12. Disputes how to be dealt with. Section 13. Undisbursed or unclaimed amount to be deposited to the general revenue account. Section 14. Repealed. Section 15. Penalties. Section 16. Offences by companies. Section 17. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 18. Delegation of powers. Section 19. Declaration as to the policy of the State. Section 20. Power to make rules. Section 21. Power to remove difficulties. Section 22. Validation. Schedule 1.
